description: Feature backlog - Product Owner owns priority

items.title: Enforce dependsOn ordering in sprint picker
items.intent: Items with unmet dependencies must never be picked before their prerequisites are done — build order must be correct or autonomous mode is unsafe.
items.description: The item schema has a dependsOn array (list of prerequisite IDs), but the sprint picker does not enforce it. In auto mode, if FEAT-002 depends on FEAT-001 but FEAT-001 isn't done yet, FEAT-002 can still be picked first, producing incorrect or incomplete implementations. The picker (find_next_item / auto-pick path) must skip any item whose dependsOn items are not all marked done before picking it. backlog check should also flag unmet dependencies as a readiness gate.
steps: In .aishore/aishore, find find_next_item() and locate where candidate items are filtered by priority scope
For each candidate item, read its dependsOn array (default empty if missing)
For each dependency ID in dependsOn, search all backlog JSON files and verify that item has status=done
Skip the candidate if any dependency is not done; move to the next candidate
Verify that items with all dependencies met are still picked normally
acceptanceCriteria: Auto-pick never selects an item whose dependsOn items are not all status: done
Items with all dependencies satisfied are still selected normally
Items skipped due to unmet dependencies are not silently lost — they remain in the backlog and will be picked once deps are done
items.groomingNotes: Steps and AC added. Picker enforcement is the runtime safety gate — companion to FEAT-003 (check-time gate). Scope: find_next_item() only.

items.title: Trigger architecture review after auto done drains backlog
items.intent: When auto done finishes, the user must get an architecture review so accumulated changes are evaluated while context is still fresh — not days later.
items.description: When auto done or auto p* fully drains all in-scope items (exit reason: all items complete, not circuit-breaker abort), aishore should offer to run an architecture review. This closes the loop on the autonomous workflow: populate → groom → auto → review. The review should be opt-in (prompt or --auto-review flag), not forced. The Architect agent already exists; this is about wiring the trigger. Also consider: after the review, the architect may add items to the backlog — auto mode should surface this as a next-step hint.
steps: In .aishore/aishore, find cmd_auto() and identify the loop exit paths: all-done vs circuit-breaker abort
Add a variable to track which exit path was taken (all-done vs abort)
On all-done exit, check for --auto-review flag; if set, skip prompt and run review directly
If --auto-review is absent, print a prompt asking the user whether to run architecture review
Invoke cmd_review (or equivalent) when confirmed
After review completes, check if new items exist in any backlog file and print a next-step hint if found
acceptanceCriteria: After auto done completes all items (not circuit-breaker abort), user is prompted to run architecture review
The --auto-review flag skips the prompt and runs architecture review automatically
No review prompt is shown when auto exits via circuit-breaker abort
If new backlog items exist after review completes, a next-step hint is printed
items.groomingNotes: Steps and AC added. Architect agent already exists; this is wiring, not building. Main risk: cleanly distinguishing all-done vs abort exit path in cmd_auto().

items.title: backlog check flags unmet dependsOn as a readiness gate
items.intent: backlog check must surface unmet dependencies so users know an item cannot safely run — dependency violations must be visible before sprint time, not discovered after.
items.description: backlog check <ID> currently validates title, intent, steps, and AC. It does not check whether items listed in dependsOn exist and are done. If an item depends on FEAT-001 and FEAT-001 is still todo, the check should warn (or fail, depending on mode). This is a companion to FEAT-001 (picker enforcement) — between the two, the system prevents dependency violations at both check-time and pick-time. Implementation: in the readiness gate logic, for each ID in dependsOn, verify the item exists in any backlog file and has status=done.
steps: In .aishore/aishore, find cmd_backlog_check() where readiness warnings are assembled
After existing checks (title, intent, steps, AC), add a loop over .dependsOn // []
For each dependency ID, call find_item (or scan backlog JSON files) to locate the item
If the dependency is not found, emit a warning: dependency ID not found in any backlog
If found but status != done, emit a warning showing the dependency ID and its current status
Ensure warning format matches other readiness gate messages
acceptanceCriteria: backlog check <ID> warns when any dependsOn item does not have status: done
Warning includes the dependency ID and its current status (or 'not found' if missing from all backlogs)
Items with no dependsOn field or an empty array pass this check without warnings
items.groomingNotes: Steps and AC added. This is the check-time companion to FEAT-001 (pick-time). Together they cover the full dependency enforcement surface. Low implementation risk — additive check in cmd_backlog_check().

items.title: Add --depends-on flag to backlog add and backlog edit
items.intent: Users must be able to declare item dependencies via the CLI — enforcing dependsOn at pick time and check time has no value if users can't set it without editing JSON by hand.
items.description: The dependsOn field exists in the item schema and is enforced by the sprint picker (FEAT-001) and backlog check (FEAT-003), but there is no CLI flag to set it. Users who want to declare that FEAT-002 requires FEAT-001 to be done first must hand-edit the backlog JSON, which is error-prone and undiscoverable. Add --depends-on <ID>[,<ID>...] to both backlog add and backlog edit. The flag should accept a comma-separated list of item IDs and write them to the dependsOn array.
acceptanceCriteria: backlog add --depends-on FEAT-001 creates an item with dependsOn: ["FEAT-001"]
backlog edit <ID> --depends-on FEAT-001,FEAT-002 sets dependsOn to both IDs, replacing any prior value
backlog show <ID> displays the dependsOn list when the field is present
items.groomingNotes: Bumped to must: FEAT-001 and FEAT-003 ship dependsOn enforcement at pick-time and check-time, but users can only set dependsOn by hand-editing the backlog JSON. That makes the enforcement features essentially unusable — users cannot express ordering constraints without violating the CLI-first design principle. This item must ship to close the usability gap left by FEAT-001 and FEAT-003. AC are concrete and user-focused.

items.title: backlog list flags items with unmet dependsOn dependencies
items.intent: backlog list must visually surface dependency blockers so users can see at a glance which items cannot be picked yet — invisible blockers cause wasted grooming and sprint failures.
items.description: backlog list currently shows items with status, priority, and readyForSprint but gives no signal that an item has unresolved dependsOn constraints. A user looking at the list cannot tell which items are blocked versus which are truly ready. The fix is to add a visual marker in the list output when an item has one or more dependsOn entries whose items are not status: done. The marker should be minimal (e.g., a [blocked] tag or a ✗ in a deps column) and only appear when blockers exist.
acceptanceCriteria: backlog list shows a visual indicator next to items whose dependsOn items are not all status: done
backlog list shows no indicator for items with no dependsOn, or when all dependencies are done
The indicator includes a count or list of blocking IDs so the user can investigate
items.groomingNotes: Kept as could. Depends on FEAT-004 being done first — visibility of blocked items only delivers value once users can actually set dependsOn via CLI. Low implementation risk (display-only change), but lower user value until FEAT-004 ships. AC are concrete.

items.title: backlog history command for querying completed sprint items
items.intent: Users must be able to query what was completed and when without parsing JSONL by hand — sprint history should be as discoverable as the backlog itself.
items.description: The archive/sprints.jsonl file records every completed sprint (itemId, date, status, duration, filesChanged, etc.) but there is no CLI command to query it. Users who want to know 'what did I complete this week?' or 'how many items passed vs failed?' must read raw JSONL. A 'backlog history' subcommand (or 'aishore history') should let users list recent completions, filter by date range or status, and surface per-item summaries. The metrics command gives aggregates; history would give the item-level view.
steps: In .aishore/aishore, find the archive path (SPRINT_ARCHIVE or sprints.jsonl) to understand the JSONL schema
Add a 'history' subcommand in the backlog command dispatcher (cmd_backlog or main case statement)

Implement cmd_backlog_history(): read sprints.jsonl, parse each line with jq, support --since YYYY-MM-DD filter (skip entries with date < arg), support --failed filter (skip entries with status == complete)
Format output as a table: ID, date, status, duration columns; align columns for readability
Add --failed flag handling: show only rows where status \!= complete
Add 'backlog history' to the help text and usage message
acceptanceCriteria: backlog history (or aishore history) lists completed sprint items with ID, date, and status
backlog history --since YYYY-MM-DD filters to items completed on or after that date
backlog history --failed shows only items where status \!= complete
items.groomingNotes: PO bump to should: we now have 20 sprint entries in the archive from today's session. Users who want to answer 'what did I complete today?' or audit a sprint run are blocked until this ships — they must read raw JSONL. The metrics command covers aggregates; history fills the item-level gap. With real data in the archive, this feature has immediate user value rather than speculative future value. Bumped from could.

items.title: Configurable auto-fix command (fix_cmd)
items.intent: Lint-fixable issues must be auto-resolved before validation.
steps: Add fix_cmd config (config.yaml, AISHORE_FIX_CMD)
Run fix_cmd after developer agent completes
Warn on failure but do not abort sprint
On retry, run fix_cmd before developer re-attempts
Tell developer agent prompt that fix_cmd will auto-run
acceptanceCriteria: fix_cmd runs after developer agent
AISHORE_FIX_CMD env var takes precedence
fix_cmd failure warns but does not abort
On retry, fix_cmd runs before re-attempt
Unchanged when fix_cmd is unset

items.title: Squash-merge feature branches into single commit
items.intent: Each sprint item must produce exactly one clean commit on the target branch.
steps: Add merge_strategy config default merge, option squash
When squash, use git merge --squash
Create commit using developer agent summary
Follow conventional commit format
acceptanceCriteria: merge_strategy squash causes squash-merge
AISHORE_MERGE_STRATEGY=squash env var works
Exactly one commit per sprint item
Squash commit includes developer summary
Default no-ff merge unchanged

items.title: Create GitHub PR instead of merging (pr flag)
items.intent: Users must be able to review sprint output before it lands on the target branch.
steps: Add pr flag to cmd_run and cmd_auto
Add create_pr config (config.yaml, AISHORE_CREATE_PR)
pr flag implies no-merge
After pushing, run gh pr create with item title and agent summaries
Fall back to no-merge if gh unavailable
acceptanceCriteria: pr flag creates a GitHub PR
PR title includes item ID and title
PR body includes agent summaries
Falls back to no-merge if gh not installed
create_pr true in config makes PR default

items.title: Failed sprint diagnostics — better crash output
items.intent: When a sprint fails, the user must see enough context to diagnose why immediately.
steps: On agent failure, tail last 20 lines of agent log
Include agent stderr if captured
Add diagnose flag for extended diagnostics
Store last failure context in .aishore/data/status/
acceptanceCriteria: Agent crash shows last 20 lines of log
diagnose flag shows extended diagnostics
Agent log path always printed on failure
Successful sprints unaffected

items.title: Git worktree isolation mode for sprints
items.intent: Sprint execution must never risk losing uncommitted work.
steps: Add isolation config default stash, option worktree
When worktree, create git worktree for sprint branch
Run developer agent in worktree directory
Merge worktree branch on success
Clean up worktree on success or failure
acceptanceCriteria: isolation worktree causes sprints in git worktree
AISHORE_ISOLATION=worktree env var works
Uncommitted changes untouched
Worktree cleaned up
Default stash behavior unchanged

items.title: Backlog sync — detect manually completed items
items.intent: The backlog must reflect reality. Items completed outside aishore should be detectable.
steps: Add backlog sync subcommand
Check todo items scope against git history
Flag items whose changes appear on current branch
Prompt to mark flagged items done
Add auto and dry-run flags
acceptanceCriteria: backlog sync lists apparently completed items
Items flagged based on scope and git history
dry-run shows candidates only
auto marks flagged items done

items.title: Item complexity gate — warn on under-specified creative work
items.intent: Creative items must be flagged to guide users toward prescriptive specs.
steps: Add complexity heuristic to backlog check
Flag items lacking file paths or with fewer than 3 steps
Suggest more prescriptive steps
Log warning before sprint
acceptanceCriteria: backlog check flags vague items
Warning is advisory not blocking
Specific items do not trigger warning
Warning suggests how to improve spec
